arXiv.title: There is No Quantum World
arXiv.comments: 15 pages. Some references added to this version, as well as some clarifying changes to section 2. Several technical edits suggested by Krzysztof Sienicki in arXiv: 2512.24198 have been incorporated into this version
arXiv.abstract: I outline a neo-Bohrian interpretation of quantum mechanics -- a view of quantum mechanics that accords with the core insights in Bohr's thinking, with a twist that justifies the prefix `neo.' In a second part of the paper, I show how von Neumann's work on infinite direct products provides a theoretical framework that deflates the measurement problem and justifies Bohr's insistence on the primacy of classical concepts.
